discoveries.title: Signal Strength Scorer is Inverted
discoveries.finding: The signal strength scorer (0-100) is inversely correlated with actual trade quality. Score 60-69: WR 51.7%, PF 2.03. Score 70-79: WR 36.5%, PF 1.09. The system currently sizes UP on exactly the wrong trades.
discoveries.action: Recalibrate scorer. Short-term: invert sizing (60-69=2c, 70-89=1c). Long-term: rebuild scorer from OOS-only component testing.

discoveries.title: Monthly-Independent Simulation Bias
discoveries.finding: The walk_forward.py and monte_carlo.py monthly-independent mode starts each month with prev_close=None, blocking all entries on day 1 of each month (neutral gap). April 2025: 0 trades in monthly mode vs 1 trade in continuous mode. April 2025 was the Trump tariff shock month (ORs 140-352pt on most days; only 4 days tradeable).
discoveries.evidence: Direct comparison: Backtester() on April 2025 alone = 0 trades; continuous from Jan 2025 = 1 trade on April 1.
discoveries.action: Fix monthly-independent simulation by seeding prev_close from prior month's last bar.

discoveries.title: April 2025 Tariff Shock — Zero Trading Month
discoveries.finding: April 2025 had 25 trading days but 0 qualifying ORB setups in monthly-independent mode. Root cause: Trump 'Liberation Day' tariffs (April 2) caused NQ OR of 140-352pt on 17/21 non-Monday days. OR_MAX filter of 110pt correctly excluded these chaos days. Only 4 days (Apr 1, 16, 25, 29) had OR in 55-110pt range.
discoveries.evidence: bar count analysis: 28,977 bars, 25 trading days. OR analysis per day in April 2025.
discoveries.action: No action needed — OR_MAX filter working correctly. Document as expected behavior during high-volatility events.

discoveries.title: Friday Day-of-Week is Weakest Non-Monday
discoveries.finding: Friday WR 35.8% (n=67), PF 1.06 — the weakest non-Monday day. Monday is skipped. Friday is borderline: PF 1.06 barely above 1.0. Skipping Fridays would remove ~20% of trades but improve average quality.
discoveries.evidence: insights.md Day of Week table (286 trades, 2022-2026)
discoveries.action: Test Friday skip hypothesis. Expected: +0.08 PF improvement, -20% trade count.

discoveries.title: Gap Dead Zone 40-60pt is Unstable Across Years
discoveries.finding: Gap 40-60pt has WR 32.5% (n=40) in aggregate, but excluding these gaps was tested and rejected: 2025 OOS improved (PF 2.22) but 2026 OOS collapsed (PF 1.17). The dead zone pattern is real but not consistent across years.
discoveries.evidence: improvement_results.md Improvement #4. Two-year OOS instability confirmed.
discoveries.action: Do not apply hard exclusion. Consider single-contract sizing for gap 40-60pt days.

discoveries.title: Consecutive Losing Days Rule is Optimal at 2
discoveries.finding: Testing MAX_CONSECUTIVE_LOSING_DAYS at 3 (PF 1.63) and 999 (PF 1.68) both performed worse than the current setting of 2 (PF 1.73). The bankroll protection of sitting out after 2 losing days outweighs the marginal WR improvement from trading.

discoveries.title: Volume Ratio Gate: Floor Works, Ceiling Doesn't
discoveries.finding: Vol ratio floor at 0.8x: flat PF (1.73). Vol ratio floor at 1.0x: worse (-0.02). BUT: vol ratio 1.5x+ WR 27.3% (n=11). A CEILING (exclude high vol) may work even though a floor didn't. High-volume OR bars are often news/reversal events.
discoveries.action: Test: exclude trades where vol_ratio > 1.5x. Hypothesis H01 in pipeline.

discoveries.title: Regime Detector Fade Mode is Dead Code
discoveries.finding: REGIME_BREAKOUT_THRESHOLD = REGIME_FADE_THRESHOLD = 0.18. Equal thresholds mean 'fade' is never returned by classify(). The fade strategy code path is permanently inaccessible in current configuration.
discoveries.action: Fix threshold asymmetry: breakout >= 0.25, fade < 0.15, skip < 0.08. Then test fade strategy OOS before enabling.

failed_strategies.name: PM VWAP Continuation
failed_strategies.reason_failed: Strong negative OOS P&L, cascades halts

failed_strategies.name: Gap Fill Strategy
failed_strategies.config: Large gap days, fade toward prior close
failed_strategies.reason_failed: Consistent OOS drag across all years

failed_strategies.name: VWAP Pullback (AM)
failed_strategies.config: Second trade after ORB, VWAP touch
failed_strategies.reason_failed: Redundant to ORB, insufficient independent edge

failed_strategies.name: London Pre-Market 3am-5am
failed_strategies.reason_failed: Max OOS PF 1.17 across all configs. Volume 4.2% of US → 4-10 tick live slippage.

confirmed_edges.name: NQ ORB Breakout v7
confirmed_edges.evidence: OOS 2025-26: PF 2.14, WR 47.2%, 72 trades. Improving YoY. MC pass 93.2%.

confirmed_edges.name: Asia Gap Continuation (CME halt)
confirmed_edges.evidence: OOS 2024-26: PF 1.80, WR 56%, 77 trades. Improving YoY: 1.42→1.82→2.31.

tested.name: Tighter Stop 20pt (25pt fixed - 5pt buffer) with 3R target
tested.description: Reduce stop from 30pt to 20pt (25pt fixed, 0pt buffer). Keep 60pt target = 3R.
tested.evidence: Current 30pt stop loses $625/trade. 20pt stop loses $405. Better RR at 3.0.
tested.note: Tighter stop = more stop-outs. Higher RR = larger wins. Net effect uncertain.

result.name: Tighter Stop 20pt (25pt fixed - 5pt buffer) with 3R target

tested.name: High-Volume Exclusion Gate
tested.description: Exclude trades where OR volume ratio > 1.5x (spike bars are noise traps).
tested.evidence: Trade memory: vol_ratio 1.5x+ WR 27.3% (n=11). Very high vol = news/reversal.

tested.name: Extended Pyramid Target (2.5R when pyramid fires)
tested.description: When pyramid fires at 1R, extend target from 2R to 2.5R to capture runners.
tested.evidence: Pyramid analysis: top 20% winners currently captured at 2R. Runners often go 3R+.
tested.note: Tests 2.5R target. May be captured by individual winners only in pyramid trades.

result.name: Extended Pyramid Target (2.5R when pyramid fires)

tested.name: Gap Dead Zone 45-55pt (tighter precision)
tested.description: The 40-60pt dead zone was unstable. Test tighter 45-55pt exclusion.
tested.evidence: Gap 40-60pt WR 32.5% (n=40). The outer bands (40-45, 55-60) may be acceptable.

tested.name: Volume Ratio Floor at 0.7x
tested.description: Skip trades where OR vol ratio < 0.7x (very low vol = low conviction).
tested.evidence: Vol <0.5x WR 45% (n=20 small). 0.5-0.75x WR 55.6% (n=9 too small). Floor tested at 0.8x: flat PF.

tested.name: Add July to Strong Months
tested.description: July 2024 produced $7,130 (8/9 wins). Add to STRONG_MONTHS.
tested.evidence: Monthly returns: 2024-07: $7,130, 8/9 wins. Brain data: Jul WR 39.1% (n=23) — borderline.
tested.note: July 2024 was unusually strong. OOS July 2025/2026 data needed to confirm.

tested.name: OR Minimum 60pt (remove borderline 55-60pt)
tested.description: Raise OR_MIN from 55 to 60 to remove the weakest ORs.
tested.evidence: OR size 55-65pt WR 42% (marginal). Sensitivity: +15% (55->63) drops PF 0.33 (FRAGILE).

tested.name: Second Breakout Re-Entry (after target hit)
tested.description: Enable SECOND_BREAKOUT_ENABLED: re-enter if price breaks again after 10:30.
tested.evidence: Feature exists but was never tested. Could capture extended trend days.

tested.name: ATR-Adaptive OR Max Filter
tested.description: Adjust OR_MAX dynamically: if rolling ATR > 200pt, allow OR up to 130pt.
tested.evidence: April 2025 (tariff shock) had ATR 350pt+; OR_MAX=110 filtered ALL 25 days.
tested.note: Tests static OR_MAX=130 as a proxy for dynamic ATR-based ceiling.

tested.name: Entry Cutoff at 10:15 ET
tested.evidence: Entry time sweep: 10:15 PF 1.97 vs 10:30 PF 2.14. Difference is 2025 vs 2026 distribution.

failed.name: Extended Pyramid Target (2.5R when pyramid fires)
